column2: Toggle display of the QuickEdit panel at PowerPoint startup.
Bullets are corrected. The style is taken from the text placeholder on the master slide. Affects: symbol, symbol/text color, paragraph indent/spacing
A shape from the stored shape templates can be inserted on the current slide.
A template from the stored slide templates can be inserted as a new slide.
Export selected slides as a separate presentation or send as e-mail
Apply selected formats from the clipboard to the selected shapes.\n\nWith CTRL the selection of formats can be edited again.
Edit and adjust selected circle segments
Edit and adjust selected process group
Convert selected process shapes into an interactive process group to easily add process steps
Convert selected shapes individually into a link

Selected shapes are arranged in a circle according to the set width/height.\nThe order of the shapes depends on the selection order: the first selected shape is positioned at 12 o'clock, the other shapes follow clockwise.
Add selected shapes to the link
Add selected shapes to the stored ID. A new link must be created first, or an existing one extended.
Add selected slides/shapes to the default favorites library. If this library does not yet exist, it is created.
'Fill' selected cells, i.e. cell content is visually repeated across the entire cell width.
Align selected cells 'Center across selection', i.e. merged cells are simulated.
Align selected cells horizontally distributed (extreme justification).
Align (position, size, rotation) of all trackers (on all slides) based on the selected tracker.

Selection of horizontal and vertical arrangement.
Selection of shapes with the same background
Selection of shapes with the same border
Selection of shapes that resemble the current shape in type/background/border
Select whether the selected, first or last shape should be used as the reference for all matching functions. The default is the currently selected shape.
Automatic selection of the arrange functions (table cells/paragraphs/shapes)
Disable automatic search for a new BKT version
Search automatically for a new version
Remove author from the document properties.
Quit and reload BKT add-in (similar to a PowerPoint restart)
Manage BKT settings, reload BKT, open website, etc.
When activated, all shapes are then aligned to the selected shape (reference shape). If no shape is selected on activation, alignment is done to the content area.
When more than 2 shapes are selected, different options can be chosen for the calculation between the center shape and all other shapes.
In the file list, also insert folders as rows
Clicking a custom style while holding the SHIFT key always creates a new shape in the chosen style.
Clicking a custom style while holding the CTRL key opens a window to edit and delete this style.

When tables and shapes are selected:
Arrange each shape that lies over a (also selected) table within the nearest table cell. The cell is determined by the shape center.

When shapes are selected:
Arrange each shape that lies completely over another (also selected) shape (=reference shape) in the nearest text paragraph within the reference shape. If fewer than 2 text paragraphs are present, arrangement is done within the entire shape.
When the height changes, the width is also changed and vice versa
When the row spacing changes, the column spacing is also changed and vice versa
For grouped shapes (toggle shapes), you can switch between the shapes within the group, i.e. only one shape of the group is ever visible. This is useful e.g. for traffic lights, scales, etc.
When the text-box inner margin of one side is changed, the inner margin of all sides is changed.
Select any Excel file and apply its theme (color and font scheme) to the current file.
Update existing agenda (based on the first agenda slide) or create agenda slides from the current slide.
Do not show certain icon fonts and exclude them from the search.

Calculates the average of all recognizable numbers in the current selection. Depending on the spell-check language, a period or comma is used as the decimal separator. Negative numbers are subtracted.
Shows the QuickEdit panel with the interactive color picker.
